articles.title: Telangana faces 10-day severe heatwave spell - Hyderabad Mail
articles.search_term: heatwave (Adilabad OR "Bhadradri Kothagudem" OR Hyderabad OR Jagtial OR Jangaon OR "Jayashankar Bhoopalpally" OR "Jogulamba Gadwal" OR Kamareddy OR Karimnagar OR Khammam OR "Komaram Bheem Asifabad" OR Mahabubabad OR Mahabubnagar OR Mancherial OR Medak OR Medchal OR Nagarkurnool OR Nalgonda OR Nirmal OR Nizamabad OR Peddapalli OR "Rajanna Sircilla" OR Rangareddy OR Sangareddy OR Siddipet OR Suryapet OR Vikarabad OR Wanaparthy OR "Warangal (Rural)" OR "Warangal (Urban)" OR "Yadadri Bhuvanagiri") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Telangana: Telangana is set to witness a sharp rise in heat intensity, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning of severe heatwave conditions across the state.
Officials at the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre said temperatures will remain above normal by two to three degrees over the next three days.
Ten districts under orange alert as heat intensifies
The IMD has issued an orange alert for 10 districts in Telangana. It has also placed the state under a red alert from April 12 to April 22, indicating a prolonged spell of extreme heat.
Adilabad, Nizamabad among worst-hit regions
Northern and eastern districts, including Adilabad, Nizamabad, Jagtial and Mulugu, may record temperatures between 44°C and 45°C. Hyderabad is also likely to see temperatures crossing 40°C.
Authorities advised people to avoid stepping out between 11 am and 5 pm and to take precautions against heat-related illnesses.
